transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Sunrise Recovery Center, this is Shelia.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hello, my name is Ann Franklin. I'm an HR manager at a company and I'm calling about one of our employees — with their knowledge and support. His name is Devon. He spoke to me this morning and asked me to help him find treatment. He's struggling with alcohol.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling, Ann, and thank you for supporting Devon in this way. Before we go further, I want to make sure I understand the consent situation correctly. Has Devon given you explicit permission to speak on his behalf and to share his information with us today?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes. He was with me this morning. He's actually in the next office right now. He's willing to talk to you directly if needed.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's actually very helpful. Given that Devon is available, it would be best if we could have him join the call for a few minutes — even briefly — so he can give verbal consent and I can ask him a couple of quick questions. Would that be possible?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, absolutely. One moment.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Of course, take your time.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi, this is Devon.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hi Devon, thank you for coming on the line. I just want to confirm — you're aware that your HR colleague Ann reached out to us on your behalf today, and you're okay with us having this conversation and sharing information about your situation?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, I am. I asked them to help me.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you, Devon. I appreciate you saying that. I have a few questions for you directly. How long have you been struggling with alcohol?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: A couple of years. It's gotten bad in the last six months.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Are you experiencing any withdrawal symptoms when you go a day without drinking?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Some anxiety and trouble sleeping. Nothing severe.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay. Devon, do you have health insurance through your employer here?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes. It's Meridian Care. My HR should have all the details.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Ann, do you have Devon's insurance information available?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes. Member ID BP91590194, group GRP-643998.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. Devon, here's what happens next: our benefits team will verify your coverage and an admissions coordinator will call you — on your direct line, not through HR unless you prefer otherwise — within two hours. Which number would you like them to use?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: My cell. I'll give it to you.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Please go ahead. And Devon — you have good people around you. That matters.

gold_labels.coaching_notes: Excellent handling of an unusual consent scenario — agent immediately asked for explicit patient consent, then asked if the patient could come on the line directly (best practice), and correctly directed the callback to the patient's personal number rather than HR. Family_caller_handling scored 5 for navigating the employer/patient/consent triangle correctly. Clinical screen limited because the handoff from HR to patient was brief.
